Friends,

When it comes to Secretary of Education Betsy DeVos, not much has changed over the last year. I was outspoken about my concerns when she was first nominated, and what I've seen over the last year has only made me more worried.
Families depend on public schools - but Secretary DeVos has proposed slashing Department of Education funding, rolled back protections for students with disabilities and blocked policies that would help students defrauded by predatory for-profit colleges.** Betsy DeVos doesn't have our students' best interests at heart. **
**I'm doing everything I can in the Senate to hold Betsy DeVos accountable, but I need to hear from 25,000 people by midnight:**
| | **Do you approve of Betsy DeVos?**

**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**

|

**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**Just when you think the Democrats couldn’t sink any lower, we find out that indeed they can! Failed gubernatorial candidate Colin Van Ostern, knowing he has NO CHANCE of beating Governor Sununu in the 2018 election cycle, has decided to primary Democrat Bill Gardner, New Hampshire’s Secretary of State.**
****
**I guess he believes that the Secretary of State should be a partisan position and he’s willing to buy the votes he needs to win the seat.**
****
**And apparently, so do the Democrat leadership in the House and Senate. This cynical attempt by the New Hampshire Democrat establishment to seek (yet another way) to destroy our election integrity is outrageous. **
****
**For the six years I served in the Senate, we battled with Democrat governors as we tried to correct years of legislative abuse of our election process. **
****
**Republicans, we are making progress in election integrity with our majorities—let’s make sure Colin Van Ostern and the Democrats don’t destroy our First-in-the-Nation Primary status and take us backwards.**
****

subject: Live Town Hall Monday 7pm ET with Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren, and Michael Moore
body:
Middle class and working class Americans used to stand a chance -- but the needs of the many have been pushed aside in favor of the very few. Now economic inequality is at unprecedented levels. And that's bad news for all of us.
[**That 's why we're so excited to partner with Sen. Bernie Sanders for a unique LIVE town hall event Monday at 7pm EST that will dig into the impact of economic inequality in the US.**](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xl4ew/6/dI/r5cs/6ROxVw/6fKlXhV7At/P/P)
Bernie will be joined by special guests Sen. Elizabeth Warren and filmmaker Michael Moore for an innovative political event designed for digital media.
**[Join us for the Inequality in America Town Hall this Monday at 7pm ET.](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xl4ew/s/dX/r5cs/6ROxVw/6o9tfv2oum/P/P)**
Following the success of a similar Medicare for All town hall in January, which drew more than 1.6 million live viewers, this event will also be broadcast exclusively through Facebook and other social media platforms. [**act.tv**](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xl4ew/v/v8/r5cs/6ROxVw/zrN5BpfPi0/P/P)is proud to be a partner along with The Guardian, NowThis, The Young Turks, and Buzzfeed.
**[Tune in to the act.tv Facebook page 7pm ET on Monday for the Town Hall stream.](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xl4ew/z/dc/r5cs/6ROxVw/skOtr13q5W/P/P)**
Bernie says he's excited to "go outside the traditional media to talk about who owns America, why the middle class is declining, [...] and how we create an economy that works for everybody, not just the 1 percent." We're excited to help him do it!

-- Harry Waisbren and the act.tv team
**_act.tv is the largest progressive video network on[Facebook](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xl4ew/U/dV/r5cs/6ROxVw/vLFq40WH01/P/P),
[Twitter](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xl4ew/j/dA/r5cs/6ROxVw/vS2jvc9I4T/P/P), and [Instagram](http://demolinks.us/c/s/EFw/xl4ew/5/d4/r5cs/6ROxVw/zKVkU6cfL7/P/P). Do more than watch!_**

If you wish to stop receiving our emails or change your subscription options, please [Manage Your Subscription](http://demolinks.us/u/OTg0MjAvMC8xOTM2Njk2LzAvMjU0LzAvNmQyYjUzMWYyNDIzMDVlZjBlYTdjNDEyODZkMzM3MWUvMTk4NzM5Mjkw)

name: The White House
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a757b5ba2c86179ccb26bdb938f6a918c58e0f6bd4bbb936f3cf100a2011e8b56615f3f06943aba633ce8501e77a23fa3479d0ed66f70971
KC, other Missouri business leaders thank Trump for tax cuts at Boeing event

- Kansas City Business Journal

During President Donald J. Trump’s visit to St. Louis on Wednesday, he talked tax cuts and regulatory rollbacks with local workers and business leaders—all while seated in front of a stunning F-15 fighter jet. The President heard from local leaders, who discussed how they were able to offer bonuses and pay raises, as well as hire more workers, because of recently passed tax cuts.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a757b5ba2c86179c1c41513b5580f7467997ba4bc5dc9571c82d35a9516c9135d5a63415e1cd09d84a2afa46da3497e6fd51ecb4df19724b

Steel manufacturer announces Frostproof plant

- The Herald Tribune

Nucor Corp. of Charlotte, North Carolina, announced plans to build a $240 million steel mill in central Florida. Sean Malott, executive director of the Central Florida Development Council, said he believed President Trump’s recent announcement on steel tariffs helped. “It’s one of the reasons the company is looking at expansion opportunities,” Malott said.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a757b5ba2c86179c224b2ea99d45426c786e64cbdf059244c81c0b3e63ef1f4ff22d31439045f9821309c1d465bbc8099b6e92914458872f
Trump Administration sanctions ‘Putin’s chef,’ other Russians over 2016 election hacking

- CNBC

On Thursday, the Trump Administration announced sanctions “against several Russian individuals and entities, including a notorious ‘troll farm,’ for their roles related to interference in the 2016 presidential campaign,” CNBC reports. These state-sponsored “troll farms” are anonymous online commentators that use disinformation and propaganda to try to influence U.S. citizens.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a757b5ba2c86179c0b2bcd16a003d32e5548400186d11a80d70d8ad82e4378c6a5b0b4b6229ca31b7edcda72e4c70e6313a6ac7b490594e0

Joy Behar publicly apologizes for calling Pence’s Christianity a ‘mental illness’

- The Washington Post

This week, daytime television personality Joy Behar issued an apology for “mocking Vice President Pence’s Christian faith and suggesting that his religious views made him mentally ill,” The Washington Post reports. “I think Vice President Pence is right; I was raised to respect everyone’s religious faith, and I fell short of that,” Behar said

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a757b5ba2c86179ccb7db81187f6c5846fc61d9228ffcf64bbdfdde351c0a00aedb625d8bc477c599b0d342f3121e1479a1edbfd41d1d58d
MS-13 member in Maryland convicted in racketeering case

- The Washington Post

On Wednesday, the U.S. Justice Department announced that a federal grand jury has found a Maryland resident guilty of racketeering conspiracy, a charge stemming from his activities in the notorious MS-13 gang. Attorney General Jeff Sessions said that Raul Ernesto Landaverde Giron’s conviction is “another victory for the American people against this uniquely barbaric gang.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a757b5ba2c86179caf16dd1a1c7cc9002c692edc8c605c96840978c93bc2eb47e9c04d946f98cffb6a657ff136efc016b748134f6fe56ee8
Pence to lay out administration views on Latin America

- The Associated Press

“Vice President Mike Pence is set to outline the administration’s views on Latin America and the Caribbean next week in a speech that lays the groundwork for President Donald Trump’s April trip to South America,” The Associated Press reports. Vice President Pence plans to use the address to call on other countries to increase the pressure on Venezuelan leader Nicholas Maduro.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a757b5ba2c86179cca9ae18cbc5435d9411618eb218ec3f1f37da68d53e69a3b241a9b6fed498494ea90e5c80d771aad5cbf0754b1c82d42
86% of university presidents cite negative effects of ‘liberal political bias on campus’: poll

- The Washington Times

“Public confidence in American universities has eroded in recent years, and campus administrators have taken notice, blaming not only the high cost of a 4-year degree but the impact of liberal bias in the academy,” Jennifer Harper writes, citing the results of a new survey of college presidents. Overall, more than 8 in 10 college and university presidents listed the perception of liberal political bias as among the contributing factors to this declining confidence.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a757b5ba2c86179c465ad1de0e8a75b78f8fbb3de5658a937a82d55fdcfd464569d3f62d0ad410095c98366ce32610aced7ee8df3178ffcf

Contact the White House |

| Happy Weekend Peter! Do you shop on Amazon? They have a super cool program called Amazon Smile that allows you to donate a percentage of every purchase you make on Amazon to My Faith Votes! That’s right. Instead of the big company choosing what charities they give to – you get to choose! **So if you want to make a difference, with each of your Amazon purchases, I encourage you to take just a minute and connect your Amazon account to My Faith Votes.** Together, we will continue to awaken more Christians to the power of Biblical principles and their votes! Amazon usually donates 0.5% of every purchase you make on smile.amazon.com to the charity of your choice, but between now and March 31st they will triple the contribution amount to 1.5%. **Here are the quick steps to ensure your Amazon purchases are going to a worthwhile cause.** 1\. Go to [https://smile.amazon.com](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsmile.amazon.com%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180317com01&n=2) [](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsmile.amazon.com&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180317com01&n=3) 2\. Type in “**My Faith Votes** ” as the organization of your choice [](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsmile.amazon.com%2Fgp%2Fchpf%2Fchange%2Fref%3Dsmi_se_rspo_change_cycsc&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180317com01&n=4) Just note, in order for the charity to receive the contribution from Amazon you must make any Amazon purchase from [https://smile.amazon.com](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsmile.amazon.com%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180317com01&n=5). Amazon.com and Smile.Amazon.com are the same company **with the same prices**. [](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsmile.amazon.com&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180317com01&n=6) **If you _already_ have an Amazon Smile Account and want to change your charity to My Faith Votes:** 1\. Go to [https://smile.amazon.com](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsmile.amazon.com%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180317com01&n=7) 2\. Click on “ _**Your Account**_ ” from the drop-down menu select “Your Amazon Smile” 3\. Click the “ _**Change Your Charity**_ ” button on the right side of your page 4\. Type in “**My Faith Votes** ” as the organization of your choice Thanks so much for taking the time to see your Amazon purchases make a difference! **United. We Stand.** Megan West

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: Fighting addiction together
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Last week, the New York Times published a story about Madison, one of Indiana's most beautiful and beloved small towns. It's a place that's home to the idyllic Clifty Falls State Park. You'll often fi |
---

| | Peter,

Last week, the _New York Times_ published a story about Madison, one of Indiana's most beautiful and beloved small towns. It's a place that's home to the idyllic Clifty Falls State Park. You'll often find it on lists of America's most romantic towns.

Sadly, the _Times_ story wasn't about Madison's historic Main Street. It was about the devastating impact opioid addiction has had on Madison's families, and the heroes waging the battle against it.

You may have heard some of the statistics by now. Opioid abuse claims more American lives than car accidents -- one every twenty-five minutes. The ripple effect addiction has on our families is one of the biggest challenges facing our state and country. But the teachers, parents, first responders, and health care providers who are fighting back have shown us that it's also an opportunity to demonstrate our resilience and compassion.

Some of my proudest moments in the Senate have been when I've been able to lend a helping hand to Hoosiers on the front lines of this fight. In July 2016, I helped to pass the _Comprehensive Addiction and Recovery Act_ , which included language I'd written making it easier for first responders to direct overdose victims to treatment and updating best practices for prescribers. In 2017, we were able to secure $10.9 million in grant funding to fight Indiana's opioid epidemic through the _21st Century Cures Act_. And just last week, my colleagues and I introduced the _Substance Use Disorder Workforce Loan Repayment Act_ to incentivize students to pursue substance abuse work so that we can get more folks on the ground in the communities that need the most help.

I wanted to write you about this issue because I know it's something that has affected nearly all of us -- from Madison, to my hometown of Granger, to Indianapolis -- and it's going to take all of us to address the problem. While I travel the state throughout this campaign, I hope to hear from some of you about how addiction may have touched your life and any ideas you have to make a difference.

Because when it comes to lending a helping hand to a neighbor, no one rolls up their sleeves like a Hoosier.

Let's get to work.

Joe |
---|---|---

|

But the teachers, parents, first responders, and health care providers who are fighting back have shown us that it's also an opportunity to demonstrate our resilience and compassion. Some of my proudest moments in the Senate have been when I've been able to lend a helping hand to Hoosiers on the front lines of this fight. In July 2016, I helped to pass the Comprehensive Addiction and Recovery Act , which included language I'd written making it easier for first responders to direct overdose victims to treatment and updating best practices for prescribers. In 2017, we were able to secure $10.9 million in grant funding to fight Indiana's opioid epidemic through the 21st Century Cures Act. And just last week, my colleagues and I introduced the Substance Use Disorder Workforce Loan Repayment Act to incentivize students to pursue substance abuse work so that we can get more folks on the ground in the communities that need the most help. I wanted to write you about this issue because I know it's something that has affected nearly all of us -- from Madison, to my hometown of Granger, to Indianapolis -- and it's going to take all of us to address the problem. While I travel the state throughout this campaign, I hope to hear from some of you about how addiction may have touched your life and any ideas you have to make a difference. Because when it comes to lending a helping hand to a neighbor, no one rolls up their sleeves like a Hoosier. Let's get to work. Joe | ---|---|--- | Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana P.O. Box 891, Indianapolis, IN 46206 ""

name: Tommy Greene
subject: Luck won't help
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | Happy St. Patrick's Day! I come from a big, Irish-Catholic family in Cleveland. Today, my friends and family are wearing t-shirts touting "the luck of the Irish". It's a fun expression, but I don't believe in leaving anything up to luck, especially our Senate seat in Ohio. **Luck won 't help Sherrod win his race.** The only way he is going to win is by building a grassroots team that can compete with special interest spending. And we can't build a grassroots team overnight. [_**We need to put in the hard work now. November will be here before we know it. Help us stay on track to reach our FEC goal.**_](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffb4c/4462dc26/4ccc5109/2000624d/3864493533/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe180317?refcode=sbe180317_m&amount=5") | | | [Donate](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffb4c/4462dc26/4ccc5109/2000624d/3864493533/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe180317?refcode=sbe180317_m&amount=5")
---
In 2012, Sherrod was called the "Hunted Democrat" because his opponents emptied their wallets trying to defeat him. They weren't able to do it though. Two words helped us win in 2012: hard work.
This time around we're going to face the same challenges. Rep. Renacci is only five points behind Sherrod -- and national Republicans and special interest groups are lining up to help him flip Sherrod's seat.
[_**But with hard work -- not luck -- we can bring home another victory in November. Help us reach all our goals from now until Election Day. Give a $5 or $10 grassroots donation toward our FEC goal.**_](http://oh.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffb4c/4462dc26/4ccc5109/2000624d/3864493533/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe180317?refcode=sbe180317_m&amount=5")
Thanks -- now let's raise some green for Sherrod!
Tommy
Tommy Greene
Deputy Finance Director

name: Tina Smith
subject: Fighting for opportunity:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
We should be proud of the fights we've won. But there's so much work left to do. | [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc49d56/c1f7015/69a7f9c7/1143c64b/726730069/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/TinaSmithMN/") Friend, There's a lot to be proud of in Minnesota. But we've still got a lot of work to do. The campaign we're building is about fighting for opportunity for all. I couldn't believe more strongly in this ideal, especially while President Trump does his best to consolidate power among the wealthiest and most connected. Just look at the tax scam that Republicans rammed through Congress late last year. Many working families will see a tax _increase_ while the wealthiest among us benefit even more. That's just not right. With Trump in the White House, winning that better future -- where "we all do better when we all do better," as Paul Wellstone said -- starts with building a grassroots campaign that can face up to the challenges ahead. To do that, I need all of you on this team: [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc49d56/c1f7015/69a7f9c7/1143c64b/726730069/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/TinaSmithMN/") I love traveling across Minnesota and hearing people's stories -- innovative small businesswomen in Stacy; Hmong community organizers in St. Paul; retirees in Duluth fighting to get the pensions they were promised; and steelworkers and firefighters, the heart of their communities, on the Iron Range. **I've found countless reasons to be proud of our state. And I also know a lot of folks who don't yet have the opportunities they deserve.** Minnesota has one of the lowest unemployment rates in the country, but I know families who work two full time jobs and still can't afford rent. Minnesota is one of the top states for access to health care, yet I know farmers who can't afford their premiums. Minnesota is proud of its inclusivity, yet women still earn less than men, and women of color have even fewer opportunities. Especially in the Trump era, we have a lot of work to do to ensure **everyone** in our community is prospering. We need an immigration system that promotes security and has the welcoming spirit that is the hallmark of our country -- starting by protecting Dreamers. We need to create good-paying jobs and make sure we've got the well-trained workforce we need to fill them. And we need to lower health care costs so people can get the care they need when they need it. There's a lot of work ahead of us. So I'm taking action. I hope you are, too. It's time to build up a grassroots force that can take on Trump Republicans who are trying to make the lives of working families so much harder. To do that, I need you on the team. Join me on Facebook to help fight for opportunity for everyone. [**Join our grassroots campaign by liking my Facebook page. It will be important for us to keep in touch about working together to win this November.**](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc49d56/c1f7015/69a7f9c7/1143c64b/726730069/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/TinaSmithMN/") Sincerely, Tina Smith | [JOIN US ON FACEBOOK »](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc49d56/c1f7015/69a7f9c7/1143c64b/726730069/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/TinaSmithMN/")
